turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi doctor. I’m here for my blood pressure check, but I’ve also been getting headaches lately.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Hello. Thanks for coming in. Let’s go through both. When did the headaches start, and where do you feel them?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: About three weeks ago. Mostly across my forehead and sometimes at the back of my head.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. How often are they happening, and how bad are they on a scale of one to ten?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Maybe three or four times a week. Not terrible, maybe a five. It’s more of a dull ache.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do they come with nausea, vomiting, blurred vision, dizziness, chest pain, or weakness anywhere?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. Just the headache. Sometimes I feel a bit tense after work.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That helps. Any fever, sinus symptoms, neck stiffness, or headaches waking you from sleep?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No fever or neck stiffness. I do not wake up with them. They usually come later in the day.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: And have you checked your blood pressure at home recently?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: A few times. It’s been around 145 over 90, sometimes a bit higher.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. Are you taking the amlodipine every day, and have you missed any doses?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Mostly yes. I may have missed one or two tablets when work was busy, but not often.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Any side effects from it, like ankle swelling, flushing, or palpitations?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, I have not noticed anything like that.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Let’s also talk about lifestyle. How is your salt intake, exercise, sleep, and stress at the moment?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I probably eat too much takeaways. I sit most of the day at work. I try to walk twice a week, but I know it is not enough. Sleep is okay, but work has been stressful.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Understood. Any chance you could be pregnant, and when was your last menstrual period?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, I’m not pregnant. My periods are still regular.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thanks. Based on what you’ve told me, this sounds most like mildly uncontrolled blood pressure with tension-type headaches, rather than anything dangerous. I’d like to check your blood pressure properly today, review your readings, and probably increase your treatment if it stays up. We should also work on salt, weight, and regular walking. If the headaches change, become severe, or you get vision changes, weakness, chest pain, or shortness of breath, seek urgent care. Otherwise, let’s arrange a follow-up soon after today’s readings.
